summary: DHH thanks several early bloggers who linked to his site during the early days of Loud Thinking. The post is a brief acknowledgment of the blogosphere's link-sharing culture circa 2001. It reflects the early web's community-driven traffic model where blogrolls and links were the primary discovery mechanism. The post is notable mainly as a historical artifact from DHH's very early blogging days, before Rails or 37signals existed.
key_points: DHH acknowledges specific bloggers who sent traffic to his new blog
Reflects the early 2000s blogosphere culture of reciprocal linking and community building
Shows DHH's blogging origins were rooted in a small, interconnected community of personal blogs
money_quotes: I'd just like to give a shout out to all the kind people who've been throwing visitors my way, in the short lifetime of my public period of loud thinking.
key_insight: A brief thank-you post from DHH's earliest blogging days, reflecting the link-sharing culture that drove discovery in the early 2000s blogosphere.
